title: Outlet registry item manifest
description: Explicit, per-item manifest (<item>.registry.json) describing a single installable registry item. The manifest is the source of truth consumed by the Outlet engine; it must never lie about the files it ships.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor tooling.

name.description: Unique item id, lowercase kebab-case (a-z, 0-9, '-'). E.g. 'email-smtp'.

type.description: Item kind. A contract is a generic port + DTOs with zero external dependency; an adapter implements a contract against a provider library.

concern.description: Infrastructure concern this item belongs to, a single lowercase word. E.g. 'email'. One concern groups one contract plus its interchangeable adapters.

description.description: Human-readable one-liner shown in the catalogue.

targetFrameworks.description: Target frameworks the item is known to build against (floor compatibility). The CLI refuses to install into a project whose TFM is not covered here.

registryDependencies.description: Other registry items this item depends on, by name (e.g. an adapter depends on 'email-abstractions'). Resolved recursively by the engine.

nugetDependencies.description: Direct NuGet dependencies. Versions are FLOORS (minimum), never locks; transitive resolution is left to NuGet. A contract item MUST declare none.

files.description: Files shipped by the item. Each path is relative to the item directory; target is the logical routing role the user's outlet.json maps to a project + namespace.

path.description: Source file path relative to the item directory, e.g. 'IEmailSender.cs'.

nugetDependencies.description: A contract item must have zero external dependencies.
